titleStmt.title: Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 736

div.p: To the respected A(lexander) by the grace of God king of the Scots, P(atrick), son of the lord P(atrick) earl of Dunbar, his sworn and loyal man, sends greeting, and duty and service in all things. I indicate to your royal excellency that I have resigned and entirely quitclaimed to God, blessed Cuthbert, and the monks of Durham serving God, Saint Mary, blessed Cuthbert and Saint Ebbe, at Coldingham, a small estate which is called Swinwood, which my ancestors

which they had owed, they withheld and appropriated, as it is to be examined through the charter of the illustrious King Edgar which T(homas), prior of Coldingham, showed to me and faithfully read through, and fully demonstrated the right of blessed Cuthbert over the same estate . Hence it is that I earnestly pray your royal excellency that moved by godly love and on account of love and reverence for the glorious confessor, Cuthbert, whom your ancestors ever loved with a special love, you be minded to confirm that (charter) to God and blessed Cuthbert and the aforesaid monks. Health and life in the Lord to your excellency!
